published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 derives closed-form bias and variance under linear interference; this lemma is a narrowed standalone specialization.

repo_prior_art.cite: No API/catalogue entry gives a bounded-support saturation-law optimality certificate; Q1 panel entries concern regression contamination, not first-stage saturation design.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 optimizes structured randomized/deterministic families; Winkler1988 and Pinelis2012 give generic moment-set geometry but not this profiled quartic design certificate.
Winkler1988 and Pinelis2012 supply the generic finite-atomic extreme-point machinery after profiling on s=m_2(nu), but not the saturation-design reduction.
CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 shows endpoint optimality can fail under interference but does not give this global iff threshold for delta_pbar.
No verified saturation-design comparator displays this explicit feasible skewed law beating the stratified benchmark in the quartic objective.

BasseFeller2016 studies finite two-stage analysis and CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 uses large-cluster/vector-law approximations, but neither states the separate mass/grid denominator realization theorem.
BasseFeller2016 and CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 do not provide an O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) optimizer-preserving rounding certificate for a continuous quartic optimum.
CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 already proves the SUTVA endpoint comparison; this is a sanity reduction plus finite-integrality split.
CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022 treats structured design families; no verified paper gives the full semialgebraic optimizer partition for the profiled quartic objective.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:anonymous-share-specialization / ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Cai-style homogeneous linear-in-share variance representation is treated as conditional setup rather than as the novelty contribution.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The global certificate, threshold, witness, and finite implementation claims are field-tier claims conditional on V(nu)=V_0+V_1 m_2+V_3 m_3+V_4(m_4-m_2^2).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Update the bibliography metadata to the published versions where available, especially Baird-Bohren-McIntosh-Ozler 2018 ReStat and Basse-Feller 2018 JRSS A, while keeping the same comparator claims.
Add section/equation anchors for CaiPougetAbadieAiroldi2022's symmetric-family optimization and SUTVA endpoint comparison so the strict-tightening claim is easier to audit.
Add a worked finite-design exhibit for the skewed witness with concrete M and m satisfying both mass and within-cluster grid constraints.
Sharpen the open-ended branch-map handle by naming the first two support-pattern cases to solve after the positive-penalty branch, so Stage 0 has a concrete algebraic route.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 as a field-tier proposal, with the accepted scope caveat binding the result to the specialized homogeneous linear-in-share variance model.
